title: Pair-Relay Composition Descriptor
description: Composition YAML describing an N-cycle pair-relay between two or more constructs. Schema covers the static, JSON-expressible portion of the validation contract from SDD §2.1.1; cross-field semantic checks (max_cycles >= sequence.length, slug resolution) are enforced by scripts/pair-relay-validate.sh and exit with code 2 on violation. See SDD §2.1 for the runtime model.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Bumped when the descriptor shape changes incompatibly.

pattern.description: Composition pattern selector. Read by compose-dispatch.sh at COMPOSITION_VALIDATE to dispatch into the RELAY_LOOP state machine.

artifact_name.description: Slug-form name of the artifact this composition produces (e.g. 'fidelity-audit-verdict'). Used in envelope filenames and orchestrator logs.

sequence.description: Ordered list of construct stages. The relay walks this sequence; max_cycles caps how many full walks occur. The same construct slug MAY appear multiple times (e.g. artisan -> crucible -> artisan).

construct.description: Loa construct slug. Resolved via construct-resolve.sh at validation time.

role.description: Slug-form role label describing what this construct does in this stage (e.g. 'declare-taste', 'validate-shipped'). Surfaced in envelope summaries.

persona.description: Optional canonical persona name (e.g. ALEXANDER, LYNCH). Null when the construct has no established persona at this role.

max_cycles.description: Hard cap on relay iterations. Cross-field rule (enforced by validator, exit 2): max_cycles >= sequence.length so at least one full walk completes.

convergence_criteria.description: Human-readable description of what 'done' looks like. Operator surfaces this at interactive checkpoints; null means 'run until max_cycles'.

surface_mode.description: Envelope-surfacing behavior. silent: jsonl only. summary (default): jsonl + <=24-line <=80-col stderr summary. interactive: + FIFO-blocking wait + WAITING-OPERATOR side-channel signal.

domain.description: Optional domain tag (e.g. 'fidelity', 'accessibility', 'frame'). Used by gecko + KEEPER lenses for cross-composition analysis.
